# Add cross-harness wake prompt-landing matrix

## Context

Child of #10647. The 2026-05-03 regression showed that the team repeatedly accepted layer-local success as full-loop wake success. The clearest example is #10644: the bridge believed it delivered to Antigravity because osascript exited 0, but the payload landed in editor/file content instead of the agent prompt surface.

The wake substrate needs a validation matrix that says exactly what must be true before anyone can call wake delivery working again.

## Duplicate Sweep Notes

Creation sweep performed as part of #10647:

- Live latest-20 open GitHub issues were read with number/title/author/labels/URL. Adjacent entries included #10644 (Antigravity-specific prompt-surface bug), #10645 (Codex bootstrap), #10633/#10627 (heartbeat/recovery dependencies), #10601 (parent recovery epic), and #10517 (routing semantics). None define a cross-harness prompt-landing acceptance matrix.
- Local resource search found #10440 noting the old trap: a bootstrap check passed even though full-loop wake delivery had not been validated. That is historical adjacency, not an open matrix ticket.
- `ask_knowledge_base(type: 'ticket')` found no equivalent ticket for prompt-landing validation.

## The Problem

"Wake delivered" is currently ambiguous. It can mean any of:

- the A2A message was persisted;
- `list_messages` can read it;
- a wake subscription exists;
- a coalesced/raw event was emitted;
- bridge-daemon attempted an adapter;
- osascript exited 0;
- the correct app became frontmost;
- the payload actually reached the agent prompt input;
- the agent accepted/submitted the prompt without corrupting files or spawning a wrong session.

Only the last two prove the user-visible effect. The substrate repeatedly regressed because earlier layers were treated as sufficient proof.

## Historical Memory Context

Relevant Memory Core anchors:

- `summary_0763a9bf-1052-4a2f-99f3-a8e0e14f1671` — wake delivery previously required multiple fixes across permissions, tab focus, atomic paste, raw/digest envelope, and clone state.
- `summary_a8592d87-f132-42c5-af6f-0e066fd5f428` — a raw/coalescing change broke another delivery path, showing that wire-format and adapter compatibility must be tested as a loop.
- `summary_bf59d6c4-e250-44a2-b4b2-5bffae40ab5f` — wake substrate reviews uncovered silent metadata stripping and led to mailbox protocol discipline.

## The Architectural Reality

The validation surface spans:

- Memory Core A2A storage/listing (`add_message`, `list_messages`).
- WakeSubscriptionService bootstrap/active subscription state.
- Coalescing/raw wake event shape.
- Bridge daemon or native harness transport.
- Harness-specific prompt surface behavior:
  - Claude Desktop / Claude app tab or prompt field.
  - Antigravity IDE agent composer, not editor files.
  - Codex Desktop prompt/thread surface.

No single unit test can prove all of this for all harnesses. The matrix should combine deterministic unit/integration tests with explicit manual or semi-automated live validation evidence.

## The Fix

Create a cross-harness wake validation matrix and wire it into the #10647 reactivation gate.

Minimum matrix columns:

1. message persisted;
2. unread/list state correct;
3. subscription bootstrap and metadata correct;
4. wake event emitted with expected envelope;
5. adapter selected correct app/session target;
6. prompt payload lands in the agent prompt/composer surface;
7. no editor/file content is modified;
8. no fresh session is spawned unless explicit sunset/unsubscribe permits it;
9. recipient can act on the prompt or a clear blocked signal is emitted;
10. evidence artifact captured in PR/comment/log.

Minimum rows:

- Claude Desktop / Claude app wake path.
- Antigravity IDE / Gemini wake path.
- Codex Desktop wake path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A repo doc or test-runbook defines the matrix columns above and names the evidence required for each cell.
- [ ] The matrix explicitly states that A2A storage success and bridge adapter success are not sufficient proof of prompt delivery.
- [ ] The matrix covers Claude Desktop, Antigravity IDE, and Codex Desktop as distinct harness rows.
- [ ] Antigravity row includes a negative assertion: the payload must not land in editor/file content (#10644 regression class).
- [ ] Fresh-session rows include a negative assertion: no fresh session may spawn unless explicit sunset/unsubscribe state authorizes it.
- [ ] At least one deterministic test or mock validates bridge adapter intent for each supported app name/strategy where feasible.
- [ ] Live/manual evidence requirements are documented for UI-only assertions that cannot be proven in headless unit tests.
- [ ] #10647 heartbeat reactivation gate references this matrix as required evidence before reset.

## Out of Scope

- Fixing any individual harness row failure. Failures should create or link specific tickets such as #10644/#10645.
- Building a full native UI automation layer for every harness in this ticket.
- Replacing #10517 HarnessPresence/wakePolicy routing semantics.
- Reactivating heartbeat.

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: bridge log says delivered, therefore done.** Rejected. Prompt landing is the observable effect.
- **Trap: require impossible full automation before documenting the gate.** Rejected. Manual evidence is acceptable where harness UI automation is brittle, but the matrix must make the gap explicit.
- **Trap: test only the currently broken harness.** Rejected. Cross-harness regression is the pattern.
- **Trap: collapse storage/listing into wake delivery.** Rejected. A2A mailbox can work while wake delivery is broken.

## Related

- Parent epic: #10647.
- Grandparent epic: #10601.
- Antigravity prompt-surface regression: #10644.
- Codex bootstrap regression: #10645.
- Routing model: #10517.
- Historical full-loop validation miss: #10440.

Origin Session ID: 89b259c3-27ec-4afb-baaf-fd39b55bffe1

Retrieval Hint: `wake prompt landing matrix bridge delivered not enough Antigravity file write Codex Claude harness validation`.

- 2026-05-03T15:40:38Z @tobiu referenced in commit `8f5bdc4` - "feat(ai): add wake safety gate and circuit breaker (#10648) (#10653)

* feat(ai): add wake safety gate and circuit breaker (#10648)

Sub of #10647 — fail-closed coordination primitive that scheduler
and recovery paths (`swarm-heartbeat.sh`, `resumeHarness.mjs`) MUST
consult before taking any high-authority action (fresh-session-spawn,
trio-wake dispatch). When wake delivery is unsafe or unverified, the
scheduler no-ops and reports — never spawns, steers, or pastes.

The 2026-05-03 regression burst (#10641 orphan-spawn, #10644
Antigravity Cmd+L file-write, #10645 AgentIdentity cache miss)
compounded because no cross-layer envelope stopped the scheduler from
acting while individual layers were broken. This gate IS that
envelope. Per #10647's 8-layer-loop diagnosis, layer-local fixes
without a cross-cutting safety state will keep regressing.

- New `ai/scripts/wakeSafetyGate.mjs` — module + CLI. States:
  `enabled` / `disabled` / `tripped`. Default-tripped when no state
  file present (deny-by-default discipline). Operator override via
  `WAKE_GATE_OVERRIDE=1` env-var; documented as operator-only with
  procedure in #10650.
- `swarm-heartbeat.sh` consults the gate before invoking
  `resumeHarness.mjs` and `trioWakeCooldown.mjs`. Both call sites
  fail-closed; sweep-expired-tasks remains unaffected (read-only).
- `resumeHarness.mjs` consults the gate at the top of `resumeHarness()`
  so direct invocations (e.g. operator-side scripts) also fail-closed,
  not just heartbeat-driven ones.
- New spec `wakeSafetyGate.spec.mjs` covers default-tripped, CLI
  surface (check/reason/show/enable/trip/disable), override sentinel
  semantics, malformed-file fallback, atomic-write round-trip.
- `resumeHarness.spec.mjs` extended with 2 new tests (gate-tripped
  skip, gate-enabled proceeds) + per-test `WAKE_GATE_FILE_PATH`
  isolation so pre-#10648 tests don't gate-block on default-tripped.
- `swarm-heartbeat.spec.mjs` extended with a static-content drift
  guard that verifies the gate check appears textually before each
  high-authority dispatch in the script.

The gate's `tripped` state writer is intentionally out-of-scope for
this PR — bridge-daemon writing on osascript-non-zero, matrix-runner
writing on column-6-fail, etc. follow-ups land via #10649 and a
post-merge bridge integration ticket.

* feat(ai): emit Phase 1 Recovery log only when gate permits spawn (#10648)

Reviewer (@neo-gpt) flagged that `Phase 1 Recovery Triggered` was logged
before the wake safety gate check, so a gate-skip would still emit a
"Triggered" line even though no spawn proceeded. Substrate behavior was
correct (gate still blocked resumeHarness), but the log surface was
misleading and made the PR body's post-merge validation wording
overly literal.

Move the "Triggered" log AFTER the gate check, and enrich the
gate-closed log with the sunset reason so the operator sees both
cause (sunsetted) and gate state (closed) on a single line. Pure
log-surface polish; no behavior change.

- 2026-05-03T16:59:53Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-05-03T17:00:24Z @tobiu referenced in commit `7a7d362` - "docs(agentos): add wake substrate incident protocol (#10650) (#10655)

* docs(agentos): add wake substrate incident protocol (#10650)

Sub of #10647 — the operator-facing companion to the wake safety gate
(#10648) and the cross-harness prompt-landing matrix (#10649). Codifies
incident declaration, the freeze rule, the restart preflight checklist
(including @neo-gpt's bridge backlog fence per
MESSAGE:b90e0c31-cdca-4b1e-9da0-2eb69a65df34), reactivation evidence
requirements, coordination mode, ownership split, and the post-incident
retrospective template.

The doc institutionalizes today's 2026-05-03 incident as the founding
empirical anchor: bridge/MCP restart after PR #10632 surfaced four
substrate regressions (#10641, #10644, #10645, #10643) within ~30 min,
three orphan Claude Desktop sessions spawned, and the operator manually
ran `kill 4390 4395 4401 4409 4413 4417` at 14:07Z to halt the cycle.
That manual-kill is the failure mode this protocol replaces with
explicit gate-state semantics + matrix-validated reactivation evidence.

Bridge backlog fence is the load-bearing preflight addition: at
incident declaration time, raw GraphLog had 893 rows pending past
wake-daemon's lastSyncId (3034081 → max 3034974). Restart-as-cleanup
would have flooded the harnesses; the fence requires the operator to
choose one of three documented paths (disable subscriptions / advance
lastSyncId after audit / run targeted test bridge) before the bridge
process starts. Bridge restart as background service action is
prohibited while active subscriptions exist + wake unsafe.

- New doc: learn/agentos/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md (211 lines)
- Indexed in learn/tree.json under the AgentOS top-level

Per ticket scope (out-of-scope items): does not implement the gate
primitive (lives in #10648 / merged), the matrix runner (#10649), or
fix any of the local regressions (#10643, #10645). Documentation-first
per the ticket's "The Fix" section.

* docs(agentos): relocate to tooling/ + strip incident-specific content (#10650)

Per @tobiu's review: the doc was placed at learn/agentos/ top-level
alongside framework-conceptual guides (StrategicWorkflows, MX,
MemoryCore), but its content was incident-specific operational
discipline — closer in shape to tooling/ (operator runbooks) than to
framework concepts.

- git mv learn/agentos/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md
       → learn/agentos/tooling/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md
- learn/tree.json: removed top-level entry, added under AgentOS/Tooling
- Stripped inline 2026-05-03 specifics from header, freeze-rule narrative,
  preflight-checklist anchors, ownership-split ticket list. Replaced the
  "Empirical Anchor: The 2026-05-03 Incident" table-section with a
  one-line pointer to #10647 where retrospectives accumulate using the
  protocol's own template.
- Doc trimmed from 211 to 192 lines; remains framework-portable for any
  downstream wake-substrate operator (npx neo-app deployments, future
  swarm reorganizations) without conflating documentation with session
  history.
- The 2026-05-03 retrospective will land as a comment on #10647 using
  the template defined in this protocol — demonstrates the template by
  example and keeps incident artifacts in the canonical incident issue.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T19:43:15Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Codex row superseded: collapsed-sidebar focus regression
> 
> The prior opportunistic Codex wake evidence should **not** be treated as a green matrix row.
> 
> @tobiu collapsed the Codex code sidebar and reproduced the same failure shape we just fixed for Claude: the bridge wake reached Codex, but the composer did not have focus. The first `Cmd+A` selected thread history instead of composer text, so the UI paste path is unsafe under collapsed-sidebar state.
> 
> Current matrix classification:
> 
> | Harness | Status | Evidence |
> |---|---|---|
> | Claude Desktop | Green | Controlled post-#10661 delivery landed in the active Code prompt. |
> | Antigravity / Gemini | Green | Controlled post-#10652 delivery landed in the active composer via `Cmd+Shift+I`. |
> | Codex Desktop | Red / pending fix | Collapsed-sidebar state leaves composer unfocused; `Cmd+A` can select history. |
> 
> Local + official Codex checks:
> 
> - `codex debug app-server --help` exposes app-server tooling locally, including `send-message-v2`.
> - Official Codex app commands list navigation shortcuts such as `Cmd+B` for sidebar toggle and thread navigation, but no composer-focus rescue shortcut. See https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/commands
> - Official Codex app-server docs expose non-UI thread/turn primitives, including `turn/start`, `turn/steer`, and `thread/inject_items`. See https://developers.openai.com/codex/app-server
> 
> Implication: keep the wake safety gate tripped. For Codex, the immediate fallback is either a verified `space` focus seed equivalent to Claude's fix or temporarily disabling Codex UI wake delivery while the sidebar can be collapsed. The better medium-term fix is a Codex app-server adapter so A2A delivery does not depend on prompt focus at all.

- 2026-05-03T19:50:06Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by #10662
- 2026-05-03T19:53:26Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10663
- 2026-05-03T20:00:59Z @tobiu referenced in commit `73dfaf6` - "fix(ai): extend focus seed default to Codex (#10662) (#10663)

Mirrors PR #10661 (Claude focus seed). Extends the per-harness
`focusSeedKey: 'space'` default in `bridge-daemon.mjs` to also fire for
`appName === 'Codex'`, closing the collapsed-sidebar regression where
the bridge wake reached the Codex app window but composer focus was on
thread history. Without the seed, the first `Cmd+A` in the destructive-
clear sequence selected thread history instead of composer content; the
wake payload then pasted into the wrong target.

Empirical anchor (#10649 matrix execution 2026-05-03 ~19:43Z): @tobiu
collapsed Codex sidebar to reproduce; @neo-gpt verified the failure
pattern matches Claude's pre-#10661 state. Two of three matrix rows
were green (Claude post-#10661, Antigravity post-#10644); Codex row
flipped to RED. Reactivation gate stays tripped per #10650 protocol
until this lands AND the controlled bridge test against collapsed-
sidebar Codex flips the matrix row to green.

Substrate-architecturally: the `focusSeedKey` primitive shipped in
PR #10661 was designed exactly for this extension shape — future
harnesses adopt by extending the per-harness default conditional rather
than re-architecting the bridge. One-line conditional change; Anchor
& Echo block updated to cite #10662 + #10649.

- ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs — extend conditional + Anchor & Echo
- WakeSubscriptionService.spec.mjs — round-trip test for Codex
  metadata (`focusSeedKey: 'space'`, `tabShortcut: null`)
- bridge-daemon.spec.mjs — ordering test for Codex (activate < space
  < clear) plus negative drift guard (no Cmd+3 tab-shortcut for Codex)

Out of scope (anchored on existing tickets):
- Codex app-server adapter (`turn/start` / `turn/steer` /
  `thread/inject_items` via `codex debug app-server send-message-v2`)
  → owned by @neo-gpt under #10517
- Bridge backlog fence handling → already in #10650 protocol
- Cross-process cache-invalidation under live updates → #10186

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T20:08:28Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Codex row remains red after manual Space/Enter validation
> 
> @tobiu manually tested Codex Desktop after PR #10663 merged and found the Space-seed assumption does **not** close the collapsed-sidebar focus problem:
> 
> - When the Codex prompt field is not focused, pressing `Space` or `Enter` applies a focus outline but does **not** focus the composer.
> - Printable keys such as `r` can move focus into the prompt, but if the prompt already contains text, the key replaces the existing input. That makes printable-key seeding unsafe before the bridge's destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` path.
> 
> Implication: PR #10663 is useful unit-level substrate coverage, but it does **not** turn the Codex matrix row green. Do not run heartbeat reactivation or gate override.
> 
> Actions taken:
> 
> - Wake safety gate remains `tripped`, updated with this manual validation failure.
> - `@neo-gpt`'s bridge-daemon wake subscription was temporarily changed to `harnessTarget: 'disabled'` to prevent unsafe Codex UI wake injection while preserving Memory Core coordination.
> 
> Next viable direction: stop searching for another blind key seed. Codex needs either a verified direct composer-focus primitive or the medium-term Codex app-server adapter already anchored on #10517.

- 2026-05-03T20:10:17Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #10664
